title: realloc Semantics and Transactional Growth · C Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar realloc semantics and transactional growth decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for amortized vector with failure invariants. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store realloc results transactionally so allocation failure preserves the original live block.
Invalidate or rebase all interior pointers and aliases when realloc may move storage.
Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Produce bounded C evidence to grow capacity without losing the old allocation on failure; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant.
